featuredCase.title: Developer adoption friction briefing
featuredCase.sourceEvidence: Quickstart completion is improving but setup remains a drop-off point.
featuredCase.baseline: Prioritize a self-serve “Setup Recovery” improvement before adding more pilot teams: publish and productize a recovery checklist/flow that explicitly handles stale CLI installs, missing environment variables, credential/workspace mismatches, and repeated setup attempts—so a failed install has a documented path to a clean state without support pasting internal steps. This directly targets the main drop-off in setup/credentials and is an explicit pilot readiness gate blocking scheduling.
featuredCase.candidate: Next product-quality improvement for pilot expansion: ship and publish a self-serve setup recovery path that covers failed installs and credential/environment issues (stale CLI, missing env vars, workspace credential mismatches, and repeated setup attempts), replacing reliance on support-pasted internal checklists. Validate it in the lab as a calm, end-to-end demo flow, and in parallel ensure generated recommendations visibly include source citations and provenance (analytics/support/interviews) to meet the pilot readiness gates before scheduling the next teams.
featuredCase.evaluatorNote: Comparison uses file-backed artifacts for the same eval case so the before/after story is inspectable.

recommendation.label: Hybrid judge comparison
recommendation.text: Use OpenAI baseline and OpenAI candidate as the current inspectable before/after story.
recommendation.warning: Hybrid LLM judge scores require manual spot checks of claim support, missing evidence, and overconfidence before claiming product improvement.
